title: Bridge status treats epoch sentinel timestamps as immediately stuck
reasoning: The same module documents and handles a literal "0" timestamp as an epoch sentinel in delivery-duration calculations, but deriveBridgeStatus uses nullish coalescing, so sentTimestamp: "0" wins over a valid recent firstSeenAt. For any in-flight transfer with this pre-indexed/race sentinel, Number("0") makes nowSeconds - ts exceed 24h and the UI overlays STUCK immediately, even when the row was first seen moments ago. That creates false operational alerts on the bridge monitor.
recommendation: In deriveBridgeStatus, parse sentTimestamp and ignore it when it is missing, non-finite, or exactly 0; then fall back to firstSeenAt before applying the stuck threshold. Keep terminal statuses unchanged.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The bridge-status tests cover missing/null sentTimestamp fallback and cover the "0" sentinel only in transferDeliveryDurationSec. They do not exercise deriveBridgeStatus with sentTimestamp: "0" and a recent firstSeenAt.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a deriveBridgeStatus test where status is SENT or PENDING, sentTimestamp is "0", firstSeenAt is now - 2h, and the expected status remains the raw in-flight status rather than STUCK.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/lib/bridge-status.ts plus the bridge-status unit test.

history.note: Bridge status now ignores epoch/invalid sentTimestamp and falls back to firstSeenAt before stuck classification; regression added.
